title: Add in-progress non-record submission for Legal TTT + Muon+ + QK Gain 4.0
author: scottcui-georgian
body:
## Summary

Adds a new in-progress non-record submission under `track_non_record_16mb`:

`2026-04-15_LegalTTT_MuonPlus_QKGain4`

This submission is derived from the base record submission `2026-03-25_ValCalib_GPTQ_XSA_BigramHash3072`, and packages the current autoresearch result generated with [`arc`](https://github.com/scottcui-georgian/arc).

Included files:
- `README.md`
- `train_gpt.py`
- `requirements.txt`
- `submission.json`

Key experiment changes relative to the base:
- legal score-first TTT
- Muon+ row-wise update normalization
- higher QK gain init (`4.0`)

Current status:
- single-seed result: `1.11314283` val_bpb
- artifact size: `17,347,791` bytes
- this is intentionally marked as an **in-progress non-record submission**, since it is currently over the 16MB limit and does not yet have a full multi-seed record-ready result

## Why include this now

The point of this submission is to document a concrete autoresearch result produced through `arc`, and to capture some practical lessons from using autoresearch on Parameter Golf:

- experiment isolation matters: separate git worktrees make logs and outcomes reproducible
- reproducibility matters: one experiment should map to one commit
- enforcing experiment structure matters: scripts, logs, hypotheses, and verdicts need a stable layout
- getting the optimization objective right matters most: autoresearch will strongly optimize whatever objective it is given
- single-agent with parallel execution worked better than multi-agent single-execution for coherent search, though breadth likely benefits from multiple agents each exploring distinct directions
submission_readme:
# Non-Record Submission (IN PROGRESS): Legal TTT + Muon+ + QK Gain 4.0

> **Status: IN PROGRESS.** Single seed (1337) completed. Artifact is 17.35 MB — over the 16 MB limit. Additional seeds and size reduction pending.

**val_bpb: 1.1131** (seed=1337, post-TTT) | **17.35 MB** (over limit) | 8×H100 SXM, 600s | Legal Score-First TTT

Base: `[2026-03-25_ValCalib_GPTQ_XSA_BigramHash3072](../../track_10min_16mb/2026-03-25_ValCalib_GPTQ_XSA_BigramHash3072/README.md)` — 1.1147 BPB (3-seed mean)

Generated by [autoresearch](https://github.com/scottcui-georgian/arc) via `arc run train`.

---

## Result (seed=1337)


| Stage               | val_bpb (exact)         | Notes                                     |
| ------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| Pre-quant (EMA)     | 1.1350                  | step 6722/20000, stopped at wallclock cap |
| Post-GPTQ int6+lzma | 1.1368 (1.13679002)     | roundtrip eval                            |
| **Post-TTT**        | **1.1131 (1.11314283)** | 3 epochs, lr=0.005, 30 chunks             |


Artifact: **17,347,791 bytes (17.35 MB)** — exceeds 16 MB limit. Not submittable as-is.

---

## Changes Over Base


|                           | Base (ValCalib) | This run                                                                               |
| ------------------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| QK gain init              | 1.5             | **4.0**                                                                                |
| Muon update normalization | none            | **row-wise F.normalize (Muon+, [arxiv:2602.21545](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.21545))** |
| Legal TTT                 | no              | **yes — score-first, 3 epochs, lr=0.005, 2M tokens/chunk**                             |
| BigramHash                | 3072×112        | 2048×128 (default, not bumped)                                                         |


Everything else (XSA-all-11, GPTQ int6+lzma, EMA+SWA, Late QAT, Parallel Muon NS5, warmdown 3500) carries over unchanged.

### Muon+ Row-wise Normalization

After Newton-Schulz5 orthogonalization, the update matrix is row-normalized (`F.normalize(update, dim=1)`) before the parameter update. This is the "Muon+" variant from [arxiv:2602.21545](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.21545). Not present in the base submission.

### Legal Score-First TTT

Each chunk of the validation set is scored under `torch.no_grad()` first, then the model trains on those already-scored tokens. No data leakage. 30 chunks × ~970K windows, 3 epochs per chunk. Reduces BPB by ~0.024 nats over the quantized baseline.

---

## arc + Autoresearch Context

This submission was generated by `[arc](https://github.com/scottcui-georgian/arc)`, the autoresearch experiment tracker and orchestrator. The arc tree for this task repo captured ~80 tracked nodes from the base 11L model through MTP, diffAttn, TTT variants, and compression stacks (int5/int6, GPTQ, LZMA, ZSTD) before landing on this stack.

### Learnings from running autoresearch with arc

**Isolation and reproducibility are non-negotiable.** Each experiment lives in its own git worktree with a dedicated commit before launch (`arc submit` auto-commits). One commit = one experiment. Without this, logs are ambiguous and results can't be reproduced or compared cleanly.

**Enforce experiment structure.** arc enforces a consistent layout — submission scripts, run logs, hypothesis notes (`arc hyp`), and verdicts (`arc result`) — so that the experiment tree stays navigable as it grows. Ad-hoc logging quickly becomes unreadable at scale.

**Getting the optimization objective right is the most important step.** Models are very good at overfitting to whatever objective they're given. If the objective is ill-defined or misaligned with the actual goal (e.g. BPB on a fixed val set vs. final leaderboard score), autoresearch outcomes are meaningless regardless of how many experiments are run. Defining and validating the objective is the first thing to get right.

**Single-agent multi-execution beats multi-agent single-execution.** Running one agent that launches multiple parallel training jobs is more reliable and cheaper to coordinate than running multiple independent agents. The agent maintains full context over the experiment tree and can route results into a coherent hypothesis board.

**But single-agent explores depth, not breadth.** A single agent will go deep into one direction (e.g. int6 compression stack) and may miss orthogonal ideas entirely. The right structure is multiple agents each owning a different research direction, each with multi-execution capability — agents explore horizontally, execution parallelizes vertically within each direction.

This is an preview of an autoresearch process in-progress.
